summary: Delivery-rating + Ships-well feature tested end-to-end. Backend pytest 11/11 PASS. Frontend: login → /orders → 'Rate delivery' → star tap (gold) → dynamic prompts (0/3/5-star) → 300-char counter → submit → 'Thanks for rating!' toast → row updates to 'You rated N★ · Edit'. Resubmit edits the rating without double-counting (verified 5→4 in UI, count stayed at 7 in seller aggregate). PDP renders 'Ships well · 4.5★ (6)' / 'Ships well · 4.6★ (7)' green badge below seller line. Fixed ONE backend bug: Order pydantic model was missing the `delivery_rating` field, so the DB-persisted rating was being stripped from GET /api/orders responses — UI could never have shown 'You rated …' without this. Added `delivery_rating: Optional[dict] = None` to `Order` in /app/backend/models.py and restarted backend.
action_items: OPTIONAL hardening: consider promoting delivery_rating to a typed pydantic submodel `DeliveryRating(stars:int, comment:str, rated_at:datetime)` instead of `Optional[dict]` — would catch malformed writes early and self-document the API.
OPTIONAL: track which buyer left each rating so abusive reviewers can be moderated (currently only stars + comment + rated_at are stored on the order doc).
Seed data created for UI testing is left behind: order id matching ^order_uitest_, seller_id=seller_ui_iter48, product_id=prod_ui_iter48 — safe to leave or delete via MongoDB.
critical_code_review_comments: Order.delivery_rating field was missing in models.py — backend persisted the rating but stripped it from API responses (fixed in this iteration).
DeliveryRatingPayload is defined inline in routers/orders.py; consider moving to models.py alongside the schema additions for consistency with the rest of the codebase.

submit_delivery_rating: per-seller increments are not idempotent across multi-seller orders if a seller doc doesn't exist — `upsert=False` means score updates silently no-op for un-seeded sellers (the resubmit branch also runs through every seller). Acceptable for now since real orders always have seller docs.
seed_data_creation: Seeded: orders.id ^order_uitest_, sellers.user_id=seller_ui_iter48 (sum=27,count=6→now 7 after UI rating), products.id=prod_ui_iter48 — used for delivered-order + Ships Well badge UI tests. Buyer buyer@example.com is bound to the seeded order.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_delivery_rating.py must be run from /app/backend (not /app) because it imports `tests.conftest`. The Order model now correctly returns delivery_rating; if any future iteration drops this field again, the /orders 'You rated N★ · Edit' UI silently regresses.
rca_of_issue: Bug: After successful POST /orders/{id}/delivery-rating, the /orders list reload didn't show 'You rated N★ · Edit' — the link stayed as 'Rate delivery'. RCA: backend wrote delivery_rating to MongoDB correctly (verified via direct query) and seller aggregates incremented (verified via /sellers/{id}/delivery-score), but FastAPI serialised the response through `Order(**o)` which dropped any field not declared on the pydantic model. The Order model had no `delivery_rating` attribute, so it was silently stripped. Fix: added `delivery_rating: Optional[dict] = None` to Order. Verified via curl + UI re-test.
